energy_price.label: Energy price → household demand + incidence
energy_price.description: Raises the consumption-tax wedge on the energy good so households face a higher energy price; with a necessity floor it yields the textbook regressive incidence.

price_source.title: Price source
price_source.description: Where the price change comes from.
price_source.notes: 'dual' = the OSeMOSYS marginal-electricity-price (commodity-balance dual) reform/base ratio — the rigorous source. 'clews_cost_index' = the cost-of-electricity index. 'controlled' = a manual % you set, for mechanism testing.

shock.title: Controlled price change
shock.description: Energy-price change as a fraction (0.20 = +20%). Used only when price_source = controlled.

recycle.title: Recycle revenue to households
recycle.description: Return the energy-tax revenue as a lump-sum transfer (approx revenue-neutral), isolating the price effect from the fiscal effect.
recycle.notes: Off = the price increase acts partly as an un-rebated tax. See guardrail recycle_or_tax.

energy_cmin.title: Energy necessity floor (c_min)
energy_cmin.description: Minimum per-household energy consumption — makes energy a necessity, producing differential exposure by income.
energy_cmin.notes: Must be calibrated BELOW every income group's baseline energy use, or the solve breaks (hard guardrail in apply()).

fuel.title: Fuel / commodity code (dual only)
fuel.description: Which CLEWS commodity to match for the dual price; null = all electricity (ELC*) codes.

use_clews_data.title: (deprecated) use CLEWS cost index
use_clews_data.description: Legacy flag superseded by price_source = clews_cost_index.

investment.label: CLEWS energy capex → public investment (crowding-out, debt)
investment.description: Routes the CLEWS power-capex increment into OG public investment (alpha_I → K_g), so the energy build competes with private capital and adds to debt.

public_only.title: Public infrastructure only
public_only.description: Route only public-infra (transmission & distribution) capex; exclude private generation capex.

target.title: Spending parameter
target.notes: alpha_bs_I is ignored by OG-Core unless baseline_spending=True (default False) — usually a silent no-op (apply() warns).

scale.title: Scale factor
scale.description: Multiply the capex increment (illustrative until the CLEWS-MUSD↔GDP deflator bridge exists).

persist.title: Permanent infrastructure
persist.description: Carry the build into the steady state (permanent) vs taper to baseline (finite transition, default).

smooth_years.title: Smoothing window (years)
smooth_years.description: Average lumpy year-to-year capex spikes over this many years.

carbon.label: Carbon price → OG fiscal revenue + CLEWS emissions penalty
carbon.description: One carbon price set as policy, feeding both an OG consumption-side tax on household energy and a CLEWS emissions penalty.

apply_to_og.title: Apply to OG (household tax)
apply_to_og.description: Levy the carbon price as an OG consumption-side tax on household energy (~1.4% of consumption; industrial carbon is unpriced on the OG side).

apply_to_clews.title: Apply to CLEWS (emissions penalty)
apply_to_clews.description: Feed the carbon price to CLEWS as an emissions penalty.

recycle.title: Recycle OG carbon revenue
recycle.description: Rebate the OG carbon revenue to households as a transfer.
recycle.notes: Off = an un-routed tax (see guardrail recycle_or_tax).

carbon_intensity.title: Carbon intensity of the energy good
carbon_intensity.description: Tonnes CO2 per unit of the OG energy good; sets the ad-valorem tax add-on.
carbon_intensity.notes: Units must be right or the tax add-on blows past 100% (apply() warns). Treat magnitudes as illustrative until calibrated.

health.label: CLEWS emissions → health → mortality / productivity
health.description: Translates the reform's emissions change into avoided (or added) deaths via an age profile, and into a productivity (morbidity) effect on effective labor.
health.status_note: ILLUSTRATIVE — the dose-response and morbidity response are PLACEHOLDERS until a GBD ambient-PM2.5 export is supplied. Do not report calibrated numbers without gbd_csv.

affects.title: Effects to include
affects.description: Mortality (recomputes the population) and/or e (productivity via effective labor).

phase_years.title: Phase-in (years)
phase_years.description: Years over which the health effect ramps in.

excess_deaths.title: Excess-deaths target (signed)
excess_deaths.description: Direct signed target; negative = lives saved. Overrides the GBD/total computation.

gbd_csv.title: GBD burden export (CSV)
gbd_csv.description: Path to a GBD ambient-PM2.5 deaths/YLD-by-age export. Supplying it retires the placeholders.

total_pollution_deaths.title: Total pollution deaths
total_pollution_deaths.description: Total attributable deaths; scaled by the emissions change to set the target.

morbidity_response.title: Morbidity productivity response
morbidity_response.description: Per-person productivity haircut at peak exposure (placeholder unless GBD YLD data is supplied).

prod_J.title: Productivity types affected
prod_J.description: Number of ability types whose effective labor the morbidity effect touches.

gbd_location.title: GBD location
gbd_location.description: Location name for the GBD lookup (defaults to the country name).

profile_path.title: Mortality age-profile file
profile_path.description: Custom mortality h(s) age profile (overrides GBD).

morbidity_profile.title: Morbidity age-profile
morbidity_profile.description: Custom morbidity g(s) age shape (overrides GBD; default uniform over active ages).

discount_rate.label: OG equilibrium rate → CLEWS discount rate
discount_rate.description: Emits the reform's OG market cost of capital as the CLEWS discount rate (rate harmonization).

rate_key.title: Rate key
rate_key.description: Which OG rate to emit (the market portfolio return r_p).

scalar.title: Single value vs path
scalar.description: Emit one averaged rate (true) or the full time path (false).

region.title: CLEWS region
region.description: Region tag to write the discount rate to.

demand.label: OG activity → CLEWS energy-service demand
demand.description: Ratio-scales CLEWS demand by the reform/base change in OG activity (the forward driver that closes the loop).
demand.status_note: Inert in a single CLEWS→OG pass (ratio ≈ 1); becomes a real driver only inside the iterated loop.

driver.title: Activity driver
driver.description: Drive demand by OG sector output (Y_m) or by the consumption good (C_i).

og_index.title: OG index override
og_index.description: Industry/good index to slice (null = the declared energy index).

clews_fuel.title: CLEWS fuel
clews_fuel.description: Target CLEWS fuel for the demand scaling.

set_investment_incentive.label: Private-capex incentive (by industry)
set_investment_incentive.description: Bias private capex in one industry by lowering its cost of capital. In OG alone this looks ~free on GDP; the payoff is the coupled CLEWS outcome.

industry.title: Target industry / resource
industry.description: Industry by name/resource (e.g. "energy") or integer index; resolved against the model's industry registry.
industry.notes: Greyed out on M=1 models and for resources the country config has not declared.

inv_tax_credit.title: Investment tax credit
inv_tax_credit.description: ITC rate on the industry's capex.

tau_b_mult.title: Corporate-tax multiplier
tau_b_mult.description: Scale the industry's corporate income tax (e.g. 0.8 = a 20% CIT cut).

phase_years.title: Phase-in (years)
phase_years.description: Apply over the first N periods (null = all).

route_revenue.label: Route a revenue/spending stream
route_revenue.description: Direct a %-of-GDP path to a fiscal destination.

pct_gdp_path.title: Amount (share of GDP)
pct_gdp_path.description: The %-GDP stream to route (a scalar or a path).

to.title: Destination
to.notes: EXOGENOUS / debt-financed — NOT a revenue-neutral recycle (for neutrality use a channel's recycle option). 'deficit' is the one neutral choice here: no param change, the debt-ratio rule absorbs it.

fill_ss_tail.title: Fill steady-state tail
fill_ss_tail.description: Carry the last value into the steady state (permanent) vs stop after the path.

energy_price.question: How does the CLEWS reform's energy price flow through to household demand and incidence?
reads_clews: energy commodity-balance dual (EBb4_*): the marginal electricity price, reform vs base
requires: a MUIOGO CBC export with the EBb4 dual in the run dirs; else set price_source: clews_cost_index
energy_price.notes: Drives the household energy wedge from the TRUE CLEWS marginal price (the OSeMOSYS LP dual), diluted by electricity's value-share of the energy good. Recycled: representing a real price via tau_c books phantom government revenue, so recycling isolates the price/substitution effect. (A rigorous resource-COST representation would use the production-input wedge -- see energy-as-production-input-spec.md.)

energy_price_controlled.question: Mechanism check: does household energy demand fall to a known +20% price?
energy_price_controlled.notes: A controlled +20% wedge (no CLEWS read), no recycle, no c_min -- the clean demand-response mechanism, not a coupled scenario.

clean_incidence.question: Who bears a +20% energy price, once you strip out the tax artifact?
clean_incidence.notes: Controlled +20% with revenue recycled (removes the phantom-tax artifact) and an energy necessity floor (c_min) so exposure is differential by income -- the textbook regressive incidence. c_min must be calibrated below every income group's baseline energy use.

investment.question: What does financing the energy build through PUBLIC investment cost (crowding-out, debt)?
reads_clews: CapitalInvestment: reform-minus-base capex on PUBLIC power infrastructure (T&D)
investment.notes: Routes only PUBLIC infrastructure (transmission & distribution) capex to public investment (alpha_I -> K_g); private generation capex is NOT public investment (use the private-capex incentive lever for that). Finite transition (tapers in the SS tail), smoothed over lumpy capex years. Magnitude illustrative until the CLEWS-MUSD <-> GDP deflator bridge exists.

carbon.question: A $50 carbon price -- recycled household-energy tax on OG, plus an emissions penalty to CLEWS.
emits_clews: EmissionsPenalty (consumed only when CLEWS re-solves -- the multi-pass seam)
carbon.notes: One carbon price set as policy. On OG it taxes HOUSEHOLD energy only (~1.4% of consumption; industrial carbon is unpriced on the OG side), recycled to households. carbon_intensity is illustrative (0.002 -> ~+10% on the energy wedge); the raw value needs the price-in-numeraire + tCO2-per-unit-good calibration (the channel hard-warns if the implied add-on exceeds 100%).

forward.question: Emit the OG->CLEWS coupling inputs (discount rate, demand) -- the producer side of the loop.
forward.notes: Post-solve channels WRITE CLEWS inputs from the OG reform; they are fed back ONLY when CLEWS re-solves (max_passes>1 with the CLEWS runner -- unbuilt). In a single pass the demand ratio is ~1 (inert) and the rate is emitted but not consumed. Use this to inspect what OG would hand CLEWS.

health.question: What is the health dividend of the CLEWS reform's cleaner air (mortality + productivity)?
requires: GBD ambient-PM2.5 burden export for a calibrated deaths total + age profile; else an illustrative placeholder
health.notes: Maps the reform's emissions change to avoided deaths (signed; cleaner air -> lives saved) via a GBD age profile, and to a productivity (morbidity) gain on effective labor. The dose-response is a PLACEHOLDER until GBD is supplied -- illustrative, not calibrated.

full.question: All six channels in one disciplined one-way pass (CLEWS->OG live; OG->CLEWS emitted).
full.notes: CLEWS->OG channels (price, investment, health) act on the reform; carbon is CLEWS-side only here (apply_to_og: false) to avoid double-wedging the energy tau_c alongside the energy-price channel. OG->CLEWS channels emit inputs for a future iterated loop.

across_steps.question: What does each channel add? energy -> +investment -> +carbon -> +health
across_steps.notes: Cumulative one-way steps sharing one baseline -- the marginal contribution of each added channel. Magnitudes ILLUSTRATIVE (recycled energy price; smoothed/scaled public capex; small carbon intensity) pending the deflator bridge; the point is the composition, not calibrated policy numbers.

guardrails.message: energy_price and carbon both add to the energy good's tau_c (when carbon applies to OG). A resource cost and a tax are different objects — ensure the underlying increase is not counted twice. (Never route the same energy cost through investment AND an I-O cost AND tau_c.)
Do not route the same transition burden through both a price/operating-cost drag and public investment (capital account).
A tau_c / carbon revenue stream must be recycled or routed, or it is an un-modeled, debt-financed fiscal expansion — not a clean price signal.
Industry/resource targeting is unavailable on single-industry (M=1) models and for resources the country config has not declared.
Flag uncalibrated magnitudes — CLEWS-MUSD vs real GDP has no deflator bridge yet (investment, carbon_intensity), and the health dose-response/morbidity response are placeholders pending GBD.
These run AFTER the reform solve; demand is inert in a single CLEWS→OG pass (it drives only inside the iterated loop).
